mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, market participants anticipate imminent monetary easing, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat when UNRATE crosses 4.0 percent, with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and retail sales signals a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth yields negative 5-day returns in late-cycle periods, with hit rate below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding 5.0 percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which suppresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL surpasses 250.0, with statistical significance at 95 percent confidence.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with mean return below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts reflect strong residential investment demand and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, with a negative rank correlation coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, each 25 basis point increase in the federal funds rate triggers immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day returns with a hit rate exceeding 55 percent.
